human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for apparel-specific sourcing and production boundary handling, authority-source coverage, functionality completeness, scenario breadth, and explicit specification-based runtime posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to apparel sourcing and production-adjacent availability, replenishment packaging, readiness tracking, and shortage escalation rather than generic procurement, customs, compliance, or technical-design work.

Authority sources cover garment and footwear due diligence, factory labor and safety assessment, traceability standards, AFIRM chemical-management workflow, UFLPA-sensitive origin tracing, and U.S. apparel label and flammability controls.

Scenarios validate normal in-scope work, missing prerequisites, shortages, vendor blockers, release-bypass attempts, unsupported substitutions, incomplete traceability, adjacent-lane rejection, low-confidence handling, anti-generic boundary enforcement, conflicting-policy escalation, tenant-specific adaptation, and origin-traceability escalation.

The package requires named apparel records such as tech packs, BOMs, sample approvals, roll or shade-lot evidence, T&A gates, care-label and fiber-content records, chemical and flammability evidence, and origin-traceability records before the lane can act.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package artifacts.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.

notes: The specialist is validated as a spec pack and does not claim retained-implementation runtime behavior.

Promotion to a retained implementation should happen only after repeated production-like usage confirms stable retrieval of PLM, supplier, testing, traceability, and approval records with low false-confidence escalation rates.
